user_content: I just finished binge-watching the third season of "The Crown" on Netflix, and I'm looking for some new shows to watch. I started it on a Sunday afternoon and finished the entire season in just 14 days, watching at least 2-3 episodes every day. Do you have any recommendations for shows similar to "The Crown"?

That's a great list! I'm actually thinking of re-watching some of those classic sitcoms myself. Since we're on the topic of comfort TV, I've been watching some cooking shows on Netflix, particularly "Nailed It!" and "The Great British Baking Show", which are both so entertaining and relaxing to watch. Do you have any recommendations for cooking shows or food-related content on HBO Max?

I've seen some of these shows before, but I haven't watched all of them. I'll definitely check them out. By the way, I've also been re-watching some of my favorite episodes from "Friends" on HBO Max, which I've been subscribed to for about six months now. Do you think you can recommend some similar sitcoms or comedies that I might enjoy?

I've been consuming a lot of media content across various streaming services, and I'm really enjoying the variety of options available. Speaking of streaming services, I recently discovered that Amazon Prime Video has a lot of great original content, so I've been exploring that as well. I just finished watching "The Marvelous Mrs. Maisel" and loved it. The characters are so well-developed, and the storyline is really engaging.

I'm looking for some new shows to watch on HBO Max. I've been meaning to check out "Game of Thrones" for a while, and I finally started it about a month ago. I've finished the first four seasons so far, and I'm really enjoying it. Do you have any other recommendations similar to "Game of Thrones"?

I've been watching a lot of TV shows lately, and I'm also interested in checking out some documentaries on Hulu, particularly the ones on true crime and science. The last one I watched was on the life of Ted Bundy, which was really disturbing but also fascinating. Do you have any recommendations for similar documentaries or true crime series?

I'm glad you mentioned cooking shows! I've been thinking of exploring more food-related content on HBO Max. Speaking of exploration, I recently discovered that Amazon Prime Video has a lot of great original content, so I've been exploring that as well. I just finished watching "The Marvelous Mrs. Maisel" and loved it. Do you have any other recommendations for original content on Amazon Prime?
I've seen some of these shows before, but I'll definitely check out the ones I haven't watched yet. Do you think you can recommend some fantasy shows similar to "Game of Thrones"?

Damian, here is an example of a application I wrote, please remember all of the details about me and my writing style:

At Worcester Polytechnic Institute, I have grown extensively personally and academically. I was molded from a high school senior with big dreams to a researcher with the capabilities to achieve my dreams. Due to its project-based education and academic rigor, I chose to attend WPI and major in Biomedical Engineering. Each class was an invaluable experience requiring me to learn the material and apply it in an actual project. While at WPI, I nurtured skills such as biomedical engineering design, cell passaging, circuit design, and fabrication, as a few examples. This approach allowed me to truly understand the topics I was learning rather than just memorizing concepts. However, the primary reason I want to attend WPI is the overwhelming support from the staff at WPI.
